g.text: Explain quantum entanglement in simple terms
Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two particles
become connected so that the state of one instantly
influences the other, regardless of distance.
Think of it like a pair of magic dice.
When you roll one die and get a 6, the other die
instantly shows 1, no matter how far apart they are.
The key insight is that this connection
happens faster than light, which Einstein called
Therefore, quantum entanglement is nature's way of
linking particles so they share a fate, no matter
where they end up in the universe.
